name: linear_tier
hypothesis: Linear-tier restraint (one concept per section, type-driven hero, mono numbers strip in lieu of unsigned customer logos, single optimization-loop visual moment, no decorative motion) closes the gap between gpt-5.5 audit (template-conforming) and Drew's verdict (which scores creative originality and density). Restraint reads as senior craft. Cutting the entire 60-second-live ExamplePanel + 'see-it' tabs + decorative pills + frameworks strip removes ~500 lines of widgetry that diluted the page; what remains is hero, proof, mechanism, products, CTA — five moments, in that order, with whitespace doing the design work.
mechanisms: Hero: removed eyebrow entirely (eyebrow 'SECURE INFRASTRUCTURE' would duplicate the H1 'Secure Infrastructure for AI'); kept the type-driven Hero.astro defaults (clamp 2.75–5.25rem, -0.025em, line-height 1.05, text-wrap balance) which were already Linear-tier; tightened subtitle to a 4-word triplet and lede to a single mechanism-dense sentence; demoted the secondary CTA from #products anchor to the docs link (a single in-page anchor on a 5-section page is dead weight).

Numbers strip replaces 'logos we hope to land' fiction: 4 real shipped numbers (0.6s p50, 671 models, 90% WebBench-50, $0.06/vCPU-hr), each its own anchor to the relevant product page, each tagged with provenance (SANDBOX SDK / TANGLE ROUTER / BROWSER AGENT). Mono tags. Numerical figures use display font with tnum and tight tracking — the figures themselves are the typographic moment, not chrome around them. depth-2 surface gives a barely-perceptible lift between hero and loop without breaking the dark spine of the page.

ONE product moment: kept the orbital optimization-loop SVG (the single most original visual asset on the existing home), moved it to a dark-surface section with a left-aligned section head, and removed everything that was competing with it — no second visual, no tabs, no streaming-output panel, no framework pill row. The loop is the page's single 'what this stack does that nothing else does' moment.

Cut the 60-second / 5-line / live-stream ExamplePanel section entirely (~280 lines). Drew killed counting headlines at R19, and the streaming-output animation was decorative motion (Linear bans). The hp-loop pulse and the orbit particle are the only motion left, both communicating real state (loop convergence).

Products section: vault surface, left-aligned head per Linear (NOT centered), mono '01 · BLUEPRINT AGENT' style numbered eyebrows (non-counting — they're indices, not 'three things'), each card is a single mechanism + a real number, the card itself is the link (no per-card CTA button chrome). Footer is a single mono '→ EVERY PRODUCT' anchor — Linear's 'footer-of-the-section' pattern.
Single-motion footer CTA: kept CtaBlock unchanged (it's already on-brand) but reduced to one button ('Open the workbench' — Linear's 'Open Linear' echo).

Color discipline: indigo accent only, used sparingly. brand-cool (#818CF8) on dark, brand-dim (#4F46E5) on vault — both AA-safe. No emerald status pills, no amber tags, no decorative gradients on cards. The only gradient is the headline 'AI' span (existing wf-gradient-text, kept for brand continuity).

Whitespace as the design: section padding stays on the canonical --section-pad-default rhythm (5rem 8%) instead of bespoke 6rem 8% per-section. Numbers strip uses 4rem vertical so it reads as a quiet aside, not a hero-equivalent moment.

AA contrast: vault section uses #4F46E5 (5.42:1 on #f4f4f9) for accents, #191c24 / #3a3a50 / #5e5e7a for text; dark sections use brand-cool / text-primary / text-secondary tokens that are scoped via global.css cascade overrides. No new hex on vault.
